name: libipc-run-perl
title: libipc-run-perl
description:
Perl module for running processes
IPC::Run allows you to run and interact with child processes using
files, pipes, and pseudo-ttys.  Both system()-style and scripted
usages are supported and may be mixed.  Likewise, functional and OO
API styles are both supported and may be mixed.
.
Various redirection operators reminiscent of those seen on common
Unix and DOS command lines are provided.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libipc-run-perl because Perl module for running processes.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libipc-run-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libipc-run-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libipc-run-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
